title: Auth — Google OAuth via NextAuth.js
problem: Band members need a shared identity layer — without creating yet another account. Google sign-in is universal in the target audience and doubles as the credential for Google Drive access.
analysis: NextAuth.js with Google provider. Scopes requested at sign-in: openid, email, profile, https://www.googleapis.com/auth/drive.file (access only to files Odio creates in the user's Drive). No Supabase. The access_token and refresh_token are stored server-side in the NextAuth session (encrypted cookie or database adapter). All Drive API calls are proxied through Next.js API routes — the token is never exposed to the client. Auth state (user email, displayName, avatar) held in Zustand. The public frozen clip page (/share/[token]) is the only unauthenticated route.
successCriteria:
- Sign in with Google works on iOS Safari, Android Chrome, and desktop
- Session persists across browser restarts (refresh token handled by NextAuth)
- All private routes redirect to /login if unauthenticated
- Sign-out clears session and redirects to landing
- drive.file scope requested at initial sign-in (needed for audio upload and clip access)
- Token refreshed automatically by NextAuth before expiry
- Auth state available synchronously on client via Zustand (no flicker)
implementation:
1. lib/auth.ts: NextAuth.js config with GoogleProvider (scopes: openid + email + profile + drive.file). PrismaAdapter with Neon Postgres. Session strategy: database.
2. app/api/auth/[...nextauth]/route.ts: standard NextAuth route handler.
3. middleware.ts: protects all routes except /, /login, /share/[token]. Redirects unauthenticated requests to /login.
4. lib/prisma.ts: singleton Prisma client.
5. Prisma schema: User, Account (NextAuth tables), Band, BandMember, JamSession, Clip, ClipVersion, Vote, Comment tables.
6. app/(auth) route group: all authenticated pages wrapped in a layout that verifies session.
7. app/login/page.tsx: sign-in page with Google OAuth button.
8. The accounts table managed by PrismaAdapter stores access_token + refresh_token per user — required for server-side Drive API calls.

decisions.decision: drive.file scope at sign-in, not deferred
decisions.rationale: drive.file is needed for all core features (upload recording, read session/clip data, write meta). Deferring it to a later step would require a second OAuth flow and would break the first recording attempt. Request it upfront with a clear explanation in the sign-in screen: 'Odio only accesses files it creates in your Drive.'
decisions.recommendation: Include drive.file in the initial Google OAuth scope request. Show a one-line privacy note on the sign-in screen.

decisions.decision: NextAuth.js with Prisma DB adapter — required from day one, not deferred
decisions.rationale: Supabase Auth adds a paid service. NextAuth.js is open-source and integrates directly with Next.js. The DB adapter (Prisma + Neon) is not optional: it is required to persist the recorder's refresh_token in the accounts table, which is the only way the audio proxy route can stream Drive audio to all band members. JWT-only session strategy is explicitly rejected — it cannot store the refresh_token server-side.
decisions.recommendation: NextAuth.js + GoogleProvider + PrismaAdapter (Neon Postgres). Session strategy: 'database'. The accounts table (managed by PrismaAdapter) holds access_token, refresh_token, expires_at for every signed-in user.

knownLimitations: drive.file scope only covers files the app created under that specific user's account — bandmates stream audio through the server-side proxy (which uses the recorder's stored token), so bandmates do not need Drive scope just to listen
Google OAuth tokens expire after 1 hour — NextAuth DB adapter stores refresh_token in Postgres and rotates on expiry; JWT-only strategy is insufficient and must not be used
If the recorder revokes Odio's Drive access (in Google account settings), all audio becomes inaccessible to the entire band until re-authorized
userGuide: Users visit the app and click 'Sign in with Google'. They are prompted to grant Google Drive access (files created by Odio only). After sign-in, they are redirected to the bands home page. Sessions persist across browser restarts. Sign-out via the Band settings page. The /share/[token] public page requires no sign-in.
